item.title: American Pleads Guilty to Working as Chinese Agent in U.S.
item.description: Thomas Pauken II is the latest in a series of prosecutions of Americans accused of secretly working for Beijing.

item.title: Xi to Leave China for First Time This Year. Destination: Pyongyang.
item.description: The visit speaks to North Korea’s rising importance among the U.S.’s biggest adversaries.

item.title: Hegseth Says U.S. Needs More From Asian Allies to Secure Balance Against China
item.description: The defense secretary adopted a softer tone on Beijing in a speech to partners concerned about the U.S.’s security commitment.

item.title: Opinion | China Gets Anxious Enough to Choose Worker Freedom
item.description: Beijing reforms its system of resident registration, which creates a domestic migrant underclass.

item.title: HSBC, StanChart Shares Slide as New Rules Could Restrict Chinese Funds in Hong Kong Accounts
item.description: Shares in London-listed financial groups with a large presence in Asia fell on fears that new rules would curtail mainland Chinese investors’ use of Hong Kong bank accounts.

item.title: China Uses LinkedIn to Lure Spy Recruits in West, U.S. and Allies Warn
item.description: Beijing is accused of expanding its use of job sites to target military and government personnel.

item.title: China’s Chip Ambitions Run Into a Global Tech Wall
item.description: Plus, Huawei will likely trail rivals by six to eight years by 2031 despite its innovations.

item.title: China Tightens Rules on Outbound Investment in Fight for Global Tech Edge
item.description: The new rules are part of Beijing’s playbook to protect technology it views as critical to its national security.

item.title: Taiwan’s Opposition Leader Comes to U.S. With a Message Straight Out of Beijing
item.description: Cheng Li-wun is set to begin a two-week U.S. tour as Beijing urges Washington to rethink its support for Taipei.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.
